Project: XNIO
Issue Type: Bug
Status: Resolved
Summary: IOException: Broken pipe errors when START_TLS is used
Description: {code} 2020-10-09 15:20:38,504 WARN [org.xnio] (default I/O-7) XNIO000307: Failed to close ssl engine when handling exception java.io.IOException: Broken pipe: java.io.IOException: Broken pipe at sun.nio.ch.FileDispatcherImpl.write0(Native Method) at sun.nio.ch.SocketDispatcher.write(SocketDispatcher.java:47) at sun.nio.ch.IOUtil.writeFromNativeBuffer(IOUtil.java:93) at sun.nio.ch.IOUtil.write(IOUtil.java:51) at sun.nio.ch.SocketChannelImpl.write(SocketChannelImpl.java:468) at org.xnio.nio.NioSocketConduit.write(NioSocketConduit.java:153) at org.xnio.ssl.JsseSslConduitEngine.doFlush(JsseSslConduitEngine.java:865) at org.xnio.ssl.JsseSslConduitEngine.handleWrapResult(JsseSslConduitEngine.java:411) at org.xnio.ssl.JsseSslConduitEngine.wrapCloseMessage(JsseSslConduitEngine.java:837) at org.xnio.ssl.JsseSslConduitEngine.closeOutbound(JsseSslConduitEngine.java:911) at org.xnio.ssl.JsseSslStreamSinkConduit.truncateWrites(JsseSslStreamSinkConduit.java:176) at org.xnio.ssl.JsseSslStreamConnection.closeAction(JsseSslStreamConnection.java:109) at org.xnio.Connection.close(Connection.java:132) at org.xnio.IoUtils.safeClose(IoUtils.java:152) at org.jboss.remoting3.remote.RemoteConnection.handleException(RemoteConnection.java:117) at org.jboss.remoting3.remote.RemoteConnection.handleException(RemoteConnection.java:104) at org.jboss.remoting3.remote.RemoteReadListener.handleEvent(RemoteReadListener.java:535) at org.jboss.remoting3.remote.RemoteReadListener.handleEvent(RemoteReadListener.java:49) at org.xnio.ChannelListeners.invokeChannelListener(ChannelListeners.java:92) at org.xnio.conduits.ReadReadyHandler$ChannelListenerHandler.readReady(ReadReadyHandler.java:66) at org.xnio.nio.NioSocketConduit.handleReady(NioSocketConduit.java:89) at org.xnio.nio.WorkerThread.run(WorkerThread.java:591) {code}

1. This problem was intruduced by XNIO-380 fix.

action_items: Investigate the impact of XNIO-380 fix on current functionality

Project: YAFT
Issue Type: Bug
Status: Open
Summary: Yaft giving error when student tries to publish
Description: We got two problems 1. when the student session is active and tries to publish, it gives You must supply some content/ subject. See the error below: org.sakaiproject.portal.api.PortalHandlerException: >java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: You must supply some content. > at >org.sakaiproject.portal.charon.handlers.ToolHandler.doPost(ToolHandler. >jav >a:73) >caused by: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: You must supply some >content. > at >org.sakaiproject.yaft.tool.YaftTool.doMessagesPost(YaftTool.java:905) > at org.sakaiproject.yaft.tool.YaftTool.doPost(YaftTool.java:565) > at javax.servlet.http.HttpServlet.service(HttpServlet.java:647) > at javax.servlet.http.HttpServlet.service(HttpServlet.java:729) > at >org.apache.catalina.core.ApplicationFilterChain.internalDoFilter(Applic >ati >onFilterChain.java:269) > at >org.apache.catalina.core.ApplicationFilterChain.doFilter(ApplicationFil >ter >Chain.java:188) > at >org.sakaiproject.util.RequestFilter.doFilter(RequestFilter.java:598) > at >org.apache.catalina.core.ApplicationFilterChain.internalDoFilter(Applic >ati >onFilterChain.java:215) > at >org.apache.catalina.core.ApplicationFilterChain.doFilter(ApplicationFil >ter >Chain.java:188) > at >org.apache.catalina.core.ApplicationDispatcher.invoke(ApplicationDispat >che >r.java:659) > at >org.apache.catalina.core.ApplicationDispatcher.processRequest(Applicati >onD 2. The student is loosing his session while working Yaft. But I am not sure where the session is going lost. see the error below: stack trace: org.sakaiproject.portal.api.PortalHandlerException: java.lang.NullPointerException at org.sakaiproject.portal.charon.handlers.ToolHandler.doPost(ToolHandler.java:73) caused by: java.lang.NullPointerException at org.sakaiproject.login.tool.LoginTool.doPost(LoginTool.java:448) at javax.servlet.http.HttpServlet.service(HttpServlet.java:647) at javax.servlet.http.HttpServlet.service(HttpServlet.java:729) at org.apache.catalina.core.ApplicationFilterChain.internalDoFilter(ApplicationFilterChain.java:269) at org.apache.catalina.core.ApplicationFilterChain.doFilter(ApplicationFilterChain.java:188) at org.sakaiproject.util.RequestFilter.doFilter(RequestFilter.java:598) ; ; ; : QueryString:null : RemoteAddress:41.221.159.83 : RemoteHost:41.221.159.83 : RemotePort:-1 : Requested URL:https://my.unisa.ac.za/portal/tool/8b3e12c3-7b45-4bed-9b7f-5f62ffbc8f31/messages : Scheme:https : ServerName:my.unisa.ac.za : Headers: : Header:accept:text/html, application/xhtml+xml, */* : Header:referer:https://my.unisa.ac.za/yaft-tool/yaft.html?state=forums&siteId=EUP1501-13-S1-390T&placementId=8b3e12c3-7b45-4bed-9b7f-5f62ffbc8f31&language=en&country=US&skin=unisa&editor=FCKeditor : Header:Accept-Language:en-ZW : Header:user-agent:Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; MSIE 9.0; Windows NT 6.1; WOW64; Trident/5.0) : Header:content-type:multipart/form-data; boundary=---------------------------7dd31a4202e4 : Header:Accept-Encoding:gzip, deflate : Header:host:my.unisa.ac.za thank you regards Vijay Unisa

1. Is this still happening?

expected.summary: The YAFT project is experiencing two critical bugs: one where students receive an error when attempting to publish without content, and another where students lose their session while working.
action_items: Investigate and resolve the content submission error when students try to publish.
Identify the cause of session loss for students using YAFT.
risks: Students may be unable to complete their work due to the publishing error.
Frequent session loss could lead to frustration and decreased user satisfaction.
open_questions: What specific conditions lead to the session loss for students?

input_text:
Project: DAOS
Issue Type: Bug
Status: Open
Summary: daos_agent GetAttachInfo unsuccessful failures
Description: Conversation with Johann: We have quite a few nodes in our cluster that have two numa nodes but only one IB interface (the second NIC is not supported in 8.4). daos_agent net-scan correctly reports ib0 on numa node 1 as the only available interface. However, in order to run ior etc successfully. we have to add the below to our daos_agent.yml file, so that the agent tells client threads on both numa nodes to share the same interface. I would argue that since daos_agent knows there is only one interface, we shouldn't have to add this configuration to the yaml file in order to make daos work. Do you agree, should I open a ticket? fabric_ifaces: - numa_node: 0 devices: - iface: ib0 domain: mlx5_0 - numa_node: 1 devices: - iface: ib0 domain: mlx5_0 Johann asked for the failure symptoms. Here is some feedback from Sherin on how the actual errors appear: [root@hl-c500 ~]# lscpu | grep NUMA NUMA node(s): 2 NUMA node0 CPU(s): 0-7,16-23 NUMA node1 CPU(s): 8-15,24-31 [root@hl-c500 ~]# ibdev2netdev mlx5_0 port 1 ==> ib0 (Up) mlx5_1 port 1 ==> ib1 (Down) [root@hl-c500 ~]# daos_agent net-scan --------- localhost --------- ------------- NUMA Socket 1 ------------- Provider Interfaces -------- ---------- ofi+tcp ib0 ofi+sockets ib0 ofi+verbs;ofi_rxm ib0 ofi+tcp;ofi_rxm ib0 ofi+verbs ib0 [root@hl-c500 ~]# daos cont create --pool stg --type posix --label stgc mgmt ERR src/mgmt/cli_mgmt.c:272 get_attach_info() GetAttachInfo unsuccessful: 2 ERROR: daos: daos_init() failed: DER_MISC(-1025): Miscellaneous error [root@hl-c500 ~]# CRT_PHY_ADDR_STR="ofi+verbs;ofi_rxm" OFI_INTERFACE=ib0 OFI_DOMAIN=mlx5_0 daos cont create --pool stg --type posix --label stgc mgmt ERR src/mgmt/cli_mgmt.c:272 get_attach_info() GetAttachInfo unsuccessful: 2 ERROR: daos: daos_init() failed: DER_MISC(-1025): Miscellaneous error Johann asked for the following: root@hl-c500 ~]# lstopo-no-graphics | grep -B 4 mlx Block(Disk) "sdad" PCIBridge PCI 83:00.0 (InfiniBand) Net "ib0" OpenFabrics "mlx5_0" PCI 83:00.1 (InfiniBand) Net "ib1" OpenFabrics "mlx5_1" ib1 is the second port in the same card as ib0, there is no cable in that port. So maybe that second port is the real problem?

expected.summary: The DAOS project is experiencing a bug where the daos_agent fails to get attach information, particularly in clusters with multiple NUMA nodes but only one supported IB interface. Configuration adjustments are required t
action_items: Open a ticket regarding the daos_agent configuration issue
risks: Failure to address the daos_agent configuration may lead to continued unsuccessful operations in the DAOS project.
open_questions: Should we consider modifying the daos_agent to automatically recognize the single IB interface without additional configuration?

input_text:
Project: WEBHOOKS
Issue Type: Bug
Status: Short Term Backlog
Summary: Webhook plugin tries to populate non-utf-8 values (0x00) to Postgres database.
Description: In some ocassions, CI/CD integrations with Bitbucket DC, will send a payload containing non-utf-8 values, such as , and the webhooks plugins will try and populate those values to the to the *AO_A0B856_HIST_INVOCATION* table on Postgres, but it fails causing the entire CI/CD pipeline to get blocked until a restart is done in Bitbucket to clear those objects from memory. The error seen in the logs is the following. {noformat} 2021-05-13 10:39:19,698 WARN [atlassian-webhooks-scheduler:thread-1] c.a.w.i.d.AsyncInvocationHistoryDao Failed to write webhook invocation data for 99:repo:refs_changed to the database com.atlassian.activeobjects.internal.ActiveObjectsSqlException: There was a SQL exception thrown by the Active Objects library: Database: - name:PostgreSQL - version:10.15 - minor version:15 - major version:10 Driver: - name:PostgreSQL JDBC Driver - version:42.2.5 org.postgresql.util.PSQLException: ERROR: invalid byte sequence for encoding "UTF8": 0x00 at com.atlassian.activeobjects.internal.EntityManagedActiveObjects.create 2021-05-13 10:39:39,680 WARN [http-nio-7990-exec-279] user5046 *6FPHBKx639x20557782x0 19tzalq ip8409,172.25.37.221 "GET /rest/api/latest/projects/ /repos/ /webhooks HTTP/1.1" c.a.w.i.d.AsyncInvocationHistoryDao Failed to flush webhook invocation data for 99:pr:merged to the database com.atlassian.activeobjects.internal.ActiveObjectsSqlException: There was a SQL exception thrown by the Active Objects library: Database: - name:PostgreSQL - version:10.15 - minor version:15 - major version:10 Driver: - name:PostgreSQL JDBC Driver - version:42.2.5 org.postgresql.util.PSQLException: ERROR: invalid byte sequence for encoding "UTF8": 0x00 {noformat} This happens because Postgres [does not allow, null (0x00) chars to be populated to text data |https://www.postgresql.org/message-id/alpine.BSO.2.00.0906031639270.2432@leary.csoft.net]. Ideally the webhooks plugin should sanitize/remove this kind of non-utf-8 data accordingly to the needs and limitations of each database.

expected.summary: The webhook plugin is encountering issues when trying to populate non-utf-8 values (0x00) into the Postgres database, causing CI/CD pipelines to block until a restart is performed.
action_items: Sanitize non-utf-8 data in webhook plugin
risks: CI/CD pipeline blockage due to invalid data being sent to the database

Project: QTWEBSITE
Issue Type: Bug
Status: Open
Summary: Qt documentation sometimes doesn't show up in searches
Description: Continuing on from QTWEBSITE-721, the search "rotationanimation qml" does not give me relevant results when searching online using startpage.com (which uses Google). There is only one doc.qt.io result, but it's for archived Qt 4.8 documentation. It should show the Qt 5 (or Qt 6; ideally, both) link: https://doc.qt.io/qt-6/qml-qtquick-rotationanimation.html
Comments:
1. Interestingly, both https://doc.qt.io/qt-6/qml-qtquick-rotationanimation.html and https://doc.qt.io/qt-5/qml-qtquick-rotationanimation.html are indexed by google, but don't show up when searching for the both terms that create the title. Maybe access to the Google site tools would tell us why this particular page got penaltized?
2. [i get https://doc.qt.io/qt-5/qml-qtquick-rotationanimator.html|https://doc.qt.io/qt-5/qml-qtquick-rotationanimator.html] for that search term on google as the second hit if I add qt 6 i get [https://doc.qt.io/qt-6/qml-qtquick-rotationanimation.html] as the first hit so it seems to be improving. is this sufficient?
3. I wouldn't call it sufficient as a user of Qt, personally. RotationAnimator is the wrong type.

expected.summary: The Qt documentation is not appearing in search results for relevant terms, specifically for 'rotationanimation qml', leading to user frustration.
action_items: Investigate Google site tools for penalty reasons
Improve search indexing for Qt 5 and Qt 6 documentation
risks: Users may become frustrated with the inability to find relevant documentation, potentially affecting adoption of Qt.
open_questions: What specific changes can be made to improve the search results for Qt documentation?

input_text:
Project: STORM
Issue Type: Story
Status: Open
Summary: Allow toggling every single projector's ability to cast a shadow separately.
Description: Since projectors were added years ago, shortly after Deferred Rendering was implemented, we were limited by the maximum amount of shadows for projectors the Viewer can simultaneously render at any given time to 2. When more than 2 projectors are present the Viewer orders them by priority to decide which one should get one of the 2 available projector shadow slots. This priority is almost exclusively dependent on the distance to your camera, this can create a lot of problems. h1. The Problem List: * We can't control the priority in any way, making the shadows appear and fade out solely depending on distance which is not always optimal. * In a room with 2 roof lights to light the room and another 2 lights on the walls to the left and/or right only the closest roof light and a light on the wall will cast a shadow, this creates the problem of the other main roof light that is suppose to cast shadows not actually casting shadows. The shadow spot is basically wasted on a wall light that is closer than the second main light. * (OPTIONAL) There's no way to control the shadows of projectors via scripts either, which would allow dynamic control based on certain criteria or even some horror game scenarios for example. h1. The Solution Adding a checkbox to the Build - Features tab besides the projector spinners similar to the base light checkbox that disables the projector shadow and sets its priority to the minimum, this will allow us to control which projectors we want to have shadows and which shouldn't. It would solve the more-than-2-projectors-room issue. It allows us to set the main roof lights to cast shadows while all others are set to not cast shadows. (OPTIONAL) It would also be nice to use the now deprecated PRIM_CAST_SHADOWS prim parameter in scripts to control this new behavior via a script allowing for even more dynamic control and some very special cases. Horror Games for example. http://wiki.secondlife.com/wiki/PRIM_CAST_SHADOWS We can also always just make a new parameter if the PRIM_CAST_SHADOW isn't descriptive enough. h1. Why is it so important? As advanced content creator i find the lack of control over projectors and their ability to cast shadows (if enabled) a deal breaker as it makes working with more than 2 projectors impossible unless you are willing to accept the visually "randomness" that the priority system appears to be, giving the free shadow spots seemingly always to the wrong 2 projectors making the scene not appear as intended. I added a picture to show this feature in action. h1. Testing The feature is currently available in the Black Dragon Viewer from 2.6.2 and onward. The actual code change for this feature can be found here: https://bitbucket.org/NiranV/black-dragon-viewer/commits/709366ab889ee481b4a15c9d20f8ed0a8cfe91d8
Comments:
1. setting up test build for evaluation
2. this JIRA https://jira.secondlife.com/browse/BUG-40914 should be linked here. basically it's the same feature request, a "Cast Shadow" toggle for a projector.
3. It might be better to have a Shadow Caster Priority property rather than a toggle, this would allow modification of the way slots are allocated and be a superset of just disabling some projectors entirely. Setting the priority to 0 would be equivalent to "Cast Shadow" being disabled, but a non-zero value could "boost" some projectors over others (so, for example, the main roof lights could be assi

expected.summary: The project aims to allow individual control over projector shadow casting, addressing limitations with the current system that restricts shadow casting to two projectors based on proximity.
action_items: Add a checkbox to the Build - Features tab for projector shadow control
risks: Current limitation of only two projectors casting shadows may lead to suboptimal lighting in scenes.
open_questions: Is there a plan to implement script control for projector shadows using the deprecated PRIM_CAST_SHADOWS parameter?

input_text:
Project: STORM
Issue Type: Story
Status: Closed
Summary: Allow toggling the Environment Map on objects separately from the actual environment reflection.
Description: For years the Viewer has been using a very basic generated Environment Map that is supposed to show some sort of reflection of the sky on a given object. This Environment Map, while it was okay back in 2007, prior to the inclusion of Basic Shaders and Windlight, it is heavily outdated and hurts the visuals of some objects more than it adds to them. h1. The Problem List: * We can't set shiny without automatically including the Environment Map, this applies to both Legacy as well as Deferred Rendering. * We can't increase the "environment" reflection slider in Build - Texture - Specular Map without automatically including the Environment Map. * The Environment Map gives objects a wrongly colored, unfitting, chromatic metallic look that shouldn't be the case for anything else other than something you actually want to look chromatic/metallic, currently this is not possible. * If i want to include the environment (e.g the projectors around an object) i will again automatically include the Environment Map, making it impossible to have projectors reflect in the object and, let's say create a fake environment map, without the Environment Map ruining the visuals again. h1. The Solution The solution is as simple as it can get, one simple checkbox that controls a boolean value in the object's parameters which tells the Viewer if it should include the Environment Map into the calculation, to prevent this from changing the look of every object that was created with this in mind, it should default to ON. There is also option B, adding a slider for the actual environment map strength, separate from the "environment" reflection slider. This would be the ultimate solution. h1. Why is this so important? As content creator i find the lack of customization for shininess extremely limiting, so much that it makes it impossible to have proper "fake" environment reflections. I'll add some pictures to show the differences between environment reflections with Environment Map and without. The images are as follows: 3937 - Environment Map On 3938 - Off 3939 - Off 3940 - Environment Map On 3941 - Off 3942 - Environment Map On 3943 - Off 3944 - Environment Map On As you can see, the record player looks better with Environment Map on while everything else looks better with it off, especially my eyes. This is why we need the option (or better a slider for full control). h1. Testing To test this change out you can download the latest Black Dragon Viewer (soon) that will include a simple toggle to enable/disable the Environment Map. The current version doesn't include one yet but instead defaults to disabling the Environment Map. With a Viewer that can toggle the Environment Map (or by commenting out the environment map inclusion in the softenLightF.glsl file, line 641 to 649) you can go to http://maps.secondlife.com/secondlife/Hippo%20Hollow/165/126/23 have a look at the projector figures, 2 tubes and 2 boxes with a bunch of projectors. The difference is quite extreme, basically broken == not broken.
Comments:
1. We decided that this is not something that we can do.

expected.summary: The project STORM addresses the outdated Environment Map used in the Viewer, which negatively impacts object visuals. A proposed solution includes adding a checkbox to toggle the Environment Map and potentially a slider
action_items: Implement checkbox to toggle Environment Map
Consider adding slider for environment map strength
risks: Failure to implement the toggle may lead to continued visual issues for content creators.
open_questions: What are the specific requirements for the checkbox and slider functionality?

input_text:
Project: STORM
Issue Type: Defect
Status: Open
Summary: FIX available - Radius calculation is wrong in viewers dating back at least 5 years
Description: h1. Steps to Reproduce Create meshes of identical size with differing numbers of faces. To make the issue obvious the Z dimension should be dominant. I have pre-created assets on aditi whcih are the ones shown in the images of the blog linked (http://beqsother.blogspot.co.uk/2016/10/bug-hunting-fixing-ancient-lod-issue.html) # face Aditi asset UUID 1 face 1d71cddb-59c5-4555-7789-7ffbdd7330a1 2 face b512d9ba-7c40-13b5-2911-db561c728f5f 3 face d3e37524-c53f-d4f2-328c-0971bf645521 4 face cf5f8206-9de5-dc28-06a5-f544f285d8de 5 face 6c60ffd2-baba-c52b-5a76-1b67bdbc0f9a 6 face bcfbf6d5-047a-d5a2-2a67-e5966921ef13 7 face fa6b61c0-5e7a-fc38-1435-cc1692152a4e 8 face 2fbd426d-9e87-2b34-d388-336b89d25c73 Line them all up and (optionally) with your LOD factor set to 1 to make the LOD shift happen sooner pull the camera back. The meshes with 3 and 4 faces should crumple immediately followed by the 5,6,7 and 8 face meshes that will crumple slightly earlier than the remaining 1 and 2 face meshes. https://gyazo.com/63f3a72cfcf3570528913efc0885ac94 There are 3 different results for the LODBias depending on the number of material faces, as this is clearly arbitrary and has nothing to do with geometry it must be considered a bug. The table of results by material count is as follows. # faces BIAS 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 h1. Actual Behavior First reported in 2011, a bug exists that results in different (and generally incorrect) LOD radius being used for Meshes depending upon the number of material faces. I have linked a number of the associated reports some of which are no longer accessible to non-Lindens. A recent example of the bug can be found documented in my blog from July http://beqsother.blogspot.co.uk/2016/07/tell-your-friends-old-bug-that-people.html Visually is is best seen in this image https://i.gyazo.com/690d132247bc8ac904600126bedd2233.gif h1. Expected Behavior As the LOD is dictated by the bounding box of the object, and not any other arbirtary detail, all mesh objects of the same scale should LOD identically. Arguably the correct bias is that shown for 5,6,7,8 as this would give half of the long diagonal of the object bounding box. h1. Other information I have a fix available but I cannot yet submit until https://jira.secondlife.com/browse/BUG-40625 has been resolved and I have completed the form. In the fix I force all mesh to have a bias equivalent to the existing behaviour for 1 and 2 material meshes as this ensures that no Mesh is adversely affected. However I suspect that strictly correct behaviour is to move everything to the 0.5 all axis bias seen on 5,6,7 & 8 material face meshes. You can read details of the fix in my blog. http://beqsother.blogspot.co.uk/2016/10/bug-hunting-fixing-ancient-lod-issue.html
Comments:
1. I have a fix for this but cannot submit until I have an agreement in place.
2. All the above are former reports of this issue which speculate (in most cases reasonably accurately) as to the cause. This jira update confirms and explains why
3. Unfortunately this patch failed Firestorm QA. I didn't build LL release with this patch, but I'm pretty certain it will have the same problem. QA should check for this bug anyway. On a viewer with this patch applied, some meshes are broken - either invisible or coloured pink or they have the incorrect texture on them. This can easily be seen on the Maitreya Lara mesh body. *Repro* * Go to http://m

expected.summary: The STORM project has an open defect regarding incorrect radius calculations in viewers that dates back at least 5 years, affecting LOD behavior based on the number of material faces.
action_items: Fix the radius calculation bug in viewers
risks: Potential user dissatisfaction due to long-standing bug affecting visual performance
open_questions: What is the timeline for implementing the fix for the radius calculation?

Project: ZOOKEEPER
Issue Type: Bug
Status: Open
Summary: Zookeeper 3.7.0 : The client supported protocol versions [TLSv1.3] are not accepted by server preferences
Description: We are trying to add TLSv1.3 support in Zookeeper, currently by default TLSv1.2 is supported. Following are the configuration {code:java} ssl.protocol=TLSv1.3 ssl.enabledProtocols=TLSv1.3,TLSv1.2 serverCnxnFactory=org.apache.zookeeper.server.NettyServerCnxnFactory sslQuorumReloadCertFiles=true quorumListenOnAllIPs=true secureClientPort=2281 sslQuorum=false portUnification=true ssl.quorum.clientAuth=need ssl.quorum.hostnameVerification=true ssl.quorum.keyStore.location=/opt/zookeeper/cert/cert1.pem ssl.quorum.trustStore.location=/opt/zookeeper/cert/cacert.pem ssl.trustStore.location=/opt/zookeeper/cert/ca/clientcacert.pem ssl.keyStore.location=/opt/zookeeper/cert/cert1.pem ssl.clientAuth=need {code} by setting "{*}ssl.enabledProtocols=TLSv1.3,TLSv1.2{*}", only TLSv1.2 communication is working but for TLSv1.3 following error coming {code:java} 2021-10-07T12:24:44.121+0000 [myid:] - ERROR [nioEventLoopGroup-4-2:NettyServerCnxnFactory$CertificateVerifier@434] - Unsuccessful handshake with session 0 x0 2021-10-07T12:24:44.123+0000 [myid:] - WARN [nioEventLoopGroup-4-2:NettyServerCnxnFactory$CnxnChannelHandler@273] - Exception caught io.netty.handler.codec.DecoderException: javax.net.ssl.SSLHandshakeException: The client supported protocol versions [TLSv1.3] are not accepted by server p references [TLS12] at io.netty.handler.codec.ByteToMessageDecoder.callDecode(ByteToMessageDecoder.java:471) ~[netty-codec-4.1.50.Final.jar:4.1.50.Final] at io.netty.handler.codec.ByteToMessageDecoder.channelRead(ByteToMessageDecoder.java:276) ~[netty-codec-4.1.50.Final.jar:4.1.50.Final] at io.netty.channel.AbstractChannelHandlerContext.invokeChannelRead(AbstractChannelHandlerContext.java:379) [netty-transport-4.1.50.Final.jar:4.1.5 0.Final] at io.netty.channel.AbstractChannelHandlerContext.invokeChannelRead(AbstractChannelHandlerContext.java:365) [netty-transport-4.1.50.Final.jar:4.1.5 0.Final] at io.netty.channel.AbstractChannelHandlerContext.fireChannelRead(AbstractChannelHandlerContext.java:357) [netty-transport-4.1.50.Final.jar:4.1.50. Final] at io.netty.channel.DefaultChannelPipeline$HeadContext.channelRead(DefaultChannelPipeline.java:1410) [netty-transport-4.1.50.Final.jar:4.1.50.Final ] at Comments:
1. Possibly the same failure but with more information: [https://evergreen.mongodb.com/task/wiredtiger_ubuntu2004_stress_tests_format_stress_test_4_aab21d5814193dc1943d9f5e62fd40ad6973475a_21_12_28_18_12_10] Backtrace: {noformat} [2021/12/29 02:58:28.739] Backtrace: [2021/12/29 02:58:28.739] /data/mci/7f8c146ecc3581f7c4debe34366c2c3d/wiredtiger/build_posix/../src/btree/bt_compact.c:241(__compact_walk
2. Again: [https://evergreen.mongodb.com/task/wiredtiger_rhel80_spinlock_gcc_test_d1e3d80d51130e3ff7003a88dc1e388090761f63_22_01_03_22_23_17] [https://evergreen.mongodb.com/task/wiredtiger_ubuntu2004_stress_tests_format_stress_test_2_9d325522ed0d1fd99266a90b0440970bbf16e940_22_01_04_04_49_20] [https://evergreen.mongodb.com/task_log_raw/wiredtiger_ubuntu2004_stress_tests_race_condition_stress_sanitize
3. What I think is happening here is the btree walk function can skip the caller's "skip" function, and if the caller is attempting to skip a class of pages, tree-walk will return a page the caller isn't prepared to handle. In this case, compact wants to skip internal pages. If a leaf page is deleted, but the delete isn't visible to the caller, tree-walk will return a leaf page to the compaction code

risks: Potential recurring segmentation fault issues affecting build stability.
open_questions: What specific changes led to the segmentation fault in the build?

input_text:
Project: WT
Issue Type: Bug
Status: Needs Scheduling
Summary: Timestamps do not work with logged tables.
Description: Recently some questions arose concerning the interaction between timestamps and logging and whether using both at once is a viable model for applications. Commits to logged tables are always immediately durable so the concept of rollback-to-stable and a stable timestamp does not apply, but it might still make sense to manage the oldest timestamp and maintain/read from history. However, this doesn't work. The major problem is that timestamp information is not written to the log. Thus, any value recovered from the log will lose its timestamp information, so if two successive values are committed and written to the log, and a crash happens before the next checkpoint, other history might remain but the first of the two will be lost. There is at least one additional problem, which is that rollback-to-stable will remove history store entries associated with logged tables if they are newer than the rollback timestamp; this would need to be changed to skip over the parts of the history store associated with logged tables. The conclusion seems to be that using timestamps and logging together is not viable and shouldn't be allowed. However, it needs to be possible to write to a logged table as part of a timestamped transaction (the oplog does this) so it isn't immediately clear what the best way to enforce this restriction is. The leading candidate so far is to drop timestamp information from updates to logged tables at the time they're created; this has a number of benefits (e.g. nothing special needs to be done on the read path, and nothing special needs to be done to avoid moving values to the history store) but might or might not turn out to actually be workable. The current documentation does not mention this combination (AFAIK) -- the questions arose as a consequence of wondering whether it should as part of other ongoing doc changes. The docs should eventually be updated once the way forward becomes clearer.
Comments:
1. [~haribabu.kommi], I'm happy to own making the changes, but would you please weigh in on this? Obviously, we'll need a careful rollout to MongoDB, but [~daniel.gottlieb]'s comments make me think there shouldn't be any problems there. cc: [~alexander.gorrod]
2. [~michael.cahill] I'd appreciate it if you can take a look at this as well. My preference is for the described solution as well - if timestamps aren't valid, getting rid of them as early as possible seems appealing. I'd also like to consider the possibility (albeit more complex) of implementing sub-transactions for this reason. That is an application could create a transaction, and then sub-transa
3. Another approach might be to use {{__wt_txn_op_set_timestamp()}} at commit time to clear logged updates to 0/MAX (and leaving the txn ID alone). But, this might result in records being written into the HS file during reconciliation, if not now, in the future?

expected.summary: The project WT has a bug related to timestamps not functioning correctly with logged tables, leading to potential data loss and issues with rollback mechanisms.
action_items: Update documentation regarding the interaction of timestamps and logging in WT
Evaluate the proposed solution of dropping timestamp information from updates to logged tables
risks: Loss of timestamp information during log recovery could lead to data inconsistency.
Rollback-to-stable may remove necessary history store entries associated with logged tables.
open_questions: What is the best way to enforce the restriction on using timestamps with logged tables?

input_text:
Project: SRCTREEWIN
Issue Type: Bug
Status: Needs Triage
Summary: Cannot Remove Keys from SSH Agent
Description: I am using SourceTree on Windows. I have SSH keys to validate the server connection. So, when I start Sourcetree it launches the SSH Agent which then asks me for the passwords for each key. That is fine. However, while I can see an option in SourceTree to "Add SSH Key" I cannot see how to remove one. My actual problem is that, due to a misleading error message, I re-added the key and it now has the same key multiple times and asks for too many passwords at start-up! But, more generally, I would like to be able to list and remove keys from the SSH agent. I have tried using command line {{ssh-add}} but it says {quote}Error connecting to agent: No such file or directory {quote} (Tried this both with windows CMD shell and in the Git bash terminal that SourceTree launches) I see a running {{ssh-agent.exe}} process in Task Manager when SourceTree is running - in the Details tab. But in the Services tab it says the service is stopped. So, I guess SourceTree has started it but how do I connect to it? Alternatively, how to tell SourceTree not to start it's own but use the Windows Service? I am using the OpenSSH SSH client (in SourceTree options). If SourceTree is managing the SSH Agent (as it seems to be) and given it gives me the option to add a key, it should either include options to remove one or to launch some other UI (or even CLI) that can do so.

expected.summary: User is experiencing issues with SourceTree on Windows where they cannot remove SSH keys from the SSH Agent, leading to multiple password prompts at startup.
action_items: Investigate options to remove SSH keys from SourceTree
Provide guidance on connecting to the running SSH Agent or using Windows Service
risks: User may face prolonged delays due to multiple password prompts at startup.
open_questions: Is there a way to list and remove SSH keys from the SSH Agent in SourceTree?
How can SourceTree be configured to use the Windows Service for SSH Agent instead of starting its own?

input_text:
Project: XNIO
Issue Type: Enhancement
Status: Closed
Summary: IoUtils#safeClose Enhancement
Description: The current IoUtils [https://github.com/xnio/xnio/blob/3.8.4.Final/api/src/main/java/org/xnio/IoUtils.java#L131-L269] already covers the broadest set of interfaces and classes which need to be closed after use. IoUtils also has the best logging concept, most util methods simply ignore exceptions. Some interfaces and classes from the JDK are missing: * javax.xml.stream.XMLStreamReader * javax.xml.stream.XMLStreamWriter * javax.xml.stream.XMLEventReader * javax.xml.stream.XMLEventWriter * javax.naming.Context * javax.naming.NamingEnumeration Only JDK8, removed in 11 * javax.xml.soap.SOAPConnection * javax.xml.ws.spi.http.HttpExchange
Comments:
1. The scope of xnio shall continue to focus on the safeClose for IO related stuff. XML and JNDI are out of scope.

expected.summary: The IoUtils#safeClose enhancement has been completed and is now closed, focusing on IO-related interfaces while excluding XML and JNDI.

input_text:
Project: XNIO
Issue Type: Feature Request
Status: Open
Summary: XNIO SelectorProvider loading via reflection is not compatible with JDK9 modules
Description: When running Undertow tests I've come across these warnings: {code} # Created at 2020-11-24T07:46:18.516 WARNING: An illegal reflective access operation has occurred # Created at 2020-11-24T07:46:18.516 WARNING: Illegal reflective access by org.xnio.nio.NioXnio$2 (file:/home/fla/.m2/repository/org/jboss/xnio/xnio-nio/3.3.8.Final/xnio-nio-3.3.8.Final.jar) to constructor sun.nio.ch.EPollSelectorProvider() # Created at 2020-11-24T07:46:18.516 WARNING: Please consider reporting this to the maintainers of org.xnio.nio.NioXnio$2 # Created at 2020-11-24T07:46:18.516 WARNING: Use --illegal-access=warn to enable warnings of further illegal reflective access operations # Created at 2020-11-24T07:46:18.517 WARNING: All illegal access operations will be denied in a future release {code} XNIO invokes the provider constructor via reflection. I think that we should invoke SelectorProvider.provider instead, unless there is a very good reason for not doing so. If there is a reason, the question that follows is if the reason applies only to JDK8, or if it applies to all JDKs. Keeping in mind that, a possible workaround is using the "java.nio.channels.spi.SelectorProvider" property to configure the specific provider according to OS, even if it has to be don in a trial and error way (ServiceConfigurationError will be thrown if the chosen provider is not available).

expected.summary: XNIO SelectorProvider loading via reflection is not compatible with JDK9 modules, leading to illegal reflective access warnings.
action_items: Investigate the use of SelectorProvider.provider instead of reflection for loading SelectorProvider.
Determine if the current reflection method is necessary for JDK8 or all JDKs.
Explore the use of 'java.nio.channels.spi.SelectorProvider' property as a workaround.
risks: Future JDK releases may deny all illegal access operations, potentially breaking functionality.
open_questions: What is the reason for using reflection in the current implementation?
Does the reason for using reflection apply to all JDK versions?

input_text:
Project: QTWEBSITE
Issue Type: Bug
Status: Open
Summary: Incorrect OpenSSL Qt 6 ANDROID_EXTRA_LIBS CMake example
Description: Under the Qt 6 site of [Using OpenSSL Libraries with Qt for Android|https://doc.qt.io/qt-6/android-openssl-support.html#using-openssl-libraries-with-qt-for-android] the CMake example for *ANDROID_EXTRA_LIBS* is incorrect and doesn't work. I suggest it should be something along the lines of: {code:none} set_property(TARGET ${PROJECT_NAME} PROPERTY QT_ANDROID_EXTRA_LIBS " /libcrypto_1_1.so;\ /libssl_1_1.so") {code}
Comments:
1. Related: [QTBUG-98754|https://bugreports.qt.io/browse/QTBUG-98754] (mine one can be closed in favor of this one)

expected.summary: The CMake example for ANDROID_EXTRA_LIBS in the OpenSSL Qt 6 documentation is incorrect and needs correction.
action_items: Correct the CMake example for ANDROID_EXTRA_LIBS in the documentation

input_text:
Project: STORM
Issue Type: Story
Status: Closed
Summary: Viewer should respect "Hide pointer while typing" Windows setting
Description: Currently, the viewer always hides the cursor while typing. However, this might not be desirable for every user. As for Windows operating systems, they come with a setting controlling this behavior (see attached screenshot). Unfortunately - like many other Windows applications as well - this setting isn't applied automatically to any application and the application has to implement this behavior explicitly, with the viewer not doing so. Proposed change is to respect the user setting made in the operating system and only hide the cursor while typing if the user has enabled this behavior. Test plan: * Check "Hide cursor while typing" option in the "Mouse" system settings * Start the viewer and type in any textbox * Cursor should be hidden while typing * Uncheck "Hide cursor while typing" option in the "Mouse" system settings while still being logged in * Type in any textbox and notice cursor is still visible * Restart the viewer and type in any textbox * Notice cursor is visible while typing Commit for proposed change: https://bitbucket.org/Ansariel/storm-2151/commits/df540da7ead66c389168bde408597ef195b9cecf
Comments:
1. Please integrate in the next available MAINT viewer
2. Change will end up in recent DRTVWR-448 P.S. This jira looks like it was assigned to me on creation, but I never saw it in "My open issues", neither received emails for it. It looks like regular assigns or assigns at creation don't add assigny into "watching" list. Upd: accidentally closed - didn't got how to move this one to qa and still no idea how to set proper state. Added jira into 448 test l

expected.summary: The viewer should respect the 'Hide pointer while typing' setting in Windows, which is currently not implemented.
action_items: Integrate the proposed change in the next available MAINT viewer
risks: The viewer does not currently respect user settings, which may lead to user dissatisfaction.
open_questions: How can the issue be moved to QA and set to the proper state?

Project: STORM
Issue Type: Story
Status: Closed
Summary: Camera presets
Description: Camera presets will operate in a similar way to graphics presets. It will save and restore settings in the Camera Controls floater. Certainly: - Orbit angle (round control on Camera Controls) - Pan location (square control on Camera Controls) - Zoom - View angle Possible additional features: - Controls related to depth of field Preferences should also include (but not save as part of a camera preset): - Opacity control for the Camera Controls floater The existing Front/Side/Rear should be put into the default camera presets, and also a "Pennys Gamer Default" Also fixed in these changes: BUG-10398 BUG-17348 BUG-17349
Comments:
1. How about we also replace SL's old default camera? Sounds like with the improved interface we'll be able to keep it as an alternate setting, but those settings were created in the 90's by Lindens with no game design experience, and they've caused a lot of problems for environment design in SL by forcing creators to build every interior room as if it were a stadium. I've attached some alternate cam
2. We should be able to what Penny suggests
3. If you mean update the default, please do. Those of us making more RL scale content have to constantly educate customers about how not to have their camera flying up behind them like a kite, and getting stuck behind walls.

expected.summary: The project STORM focuses on implementing camera presets that will save and restore settings in the Camera Controls floater, with additional features and improvements to existing defaults.
action_items: Implement camera presets functionality
Update the default camera settings
risks: Old default camera settings may continue to cause issues for environment design.
open_questions: Should we replace SL's old default camera entirely?

input_text:
Project: CONJ
Issue Type: Bug
Status: Closed
Summary: Read-only transaction with auto-commit=false not committed when using Master/slave or Aurora configuration
Description: *Setup* - Aurora cluster with Master with READ_COMMITTED isolation level Read replica (uses REPEATABLE_READ isolation by default in Aurora) - We set auto-commit to false on the Hikari connection pool - We use Spring with @Transactional(readOnly=true) {code:java} @Transactional(readOnly=true) public String getCount() { return jdbcTemplate.executeQuery("SELECT count(*) FROM some_table", ...); } {code} *Issue:* When we execute the getCount method multiple times we always get the same count, even though items are added to the some_table between invocations of getCount. *Findings* This is caused by not performing a commit after the invocation of getCount. In the MariaDbConnection.commit method the protocol.inTransaction() returns false. So the st.execute("COMMIT") is never actually invoked, although Spring tries to commit the transaction. Since Aurora read instances are always REPEATABLE_READ, and we use auto-commit false, the transaction will be kept alive without a COMMIT, which explains the stale date. When we use driver version 2.3.0 we don't see this issue since protocol.inTransaction() correctly indicates the transaction is active, and a commit occurs at the end of the getCount method. Starting from version 2.4.0 we see this erroneous behaviour. We have currently reverted back to version 2.3.0.

expected.summary: A bug was identified in the transaction handling of a read-only method in an Aurora cluster setup, where the transaction does not commit due to the driver version change.
action_items: Revert to driver version 2.3.0
Investigate the issue with driver version 2.4.0
risks: Stale data being read due to uncommitted transactions in the Aurora setup.
open_questions: What changes in driver version 2.4.0 caused the commit issue?

input_text:
Project: CONJ
Issue Type: Bug
Status: Closed
Summary: An SQL statement suddenly fails to execute
Description: When the system consumes the message in SQS, it is ready to update a record, the result of the update fails to report an error, and then it is always reported error during repeated consumption (this SQL statement should be executed very quickly, just update a field according to the primary key id). As a result, the SQS is pressed a lot of messages and cannot be consumed until the service is restarted, and the message in the queue is consumed. At this point the database is normal, other data queries and inserts are normal. The error message is as follows ### The error may exist in com/test/repository/ProductRepository.java (mapper) ### The error may involve com.test.repository.ProductRepository.__update_by_id_ignore_null-Inline ### The error occurred while setting parameters ### SQL: update product_sales SET end_date=? where id = ? ### Cause: java.sql.SQLNonTransientConnectionException: (conn=581116) Communications link failure with primary host rds-other.production.net:3306. Connection timed out ; (conn=581116) Communications link failure with primary host rds-other.production.net:3306. Connection timed out; nested exception is java.sql.SQLNonTransientConnectionException: (conn=581116) Communications link failure with primary host rds-other.production.net:3306. Connection timed out See the file for complete error messages.

expected.summary: An SQL statement fails to execute during message consumption from SQS, causing a backlog of messages until the service is restarted.
action_items: Investigate the SQL execution failure and connection timeout issue.
risks: Repeated SQL execution failures may lead to service downtime and message backlog.
open_questions: What measures can be implemented to prevent SQL execution failures in the future?

Project: YAFT
Issue Type: Feature Request
Status: Open
Summary: Ability to organize the topics in the list
Description: Umeå university in Sweden has around 60 course-sites per year that uses Yaft. One feature our faculty is asking for is the ability to arrange (and lock) the order of topics. This would mean that we have a usecase where students should not be allowed to sort the order of topics. So: Teacher or Maintain should somehow be able to order/re-order/organize topics and also have the option to _not_ allow students or access to order/re-order/organize the list of topics.
Comments:
1. [~accountid:6164e7e764ff010071b826bb] So, you're thinking of some kind of drag and drop function on the topics? Would you want the same in forums - I expect you would.

expected.summary: Feature request for YAFT to allow teachers to organize and lock the order of topics in course sites, preventing students from rearranging them.
action_items: Implement topic organization feature
open_questions: Should the drag and drop function be extended to forums as well?

input_text:
Project: TFSAP
Issue Type: Bug
Status: Open
Summary: Reject artifact property changes if no repository is specified
Description: The ADS task [ArtifactoryProperties@1 |https://www.jfrog.com/confluence/display/JFROG/Artifactory+Azure+DevOps+Extension#ArtifactoryAzureDevOpsExtension-SettingandDeletingPropertiesonFilesinArtifactory] can be used without specifying a repository in the `pattern` argument. This means that you can apply your changes to all artifacts that match the given wildcard expression everywhere in Artifactory. As an example, a developer accidentally called the task like this: {code:java} - task: ArtifactoryProperties@1 inputs: command: 'set' artifactoryService: 'Artifactory' setProps: 'someproperty=1;' specSource: 'taskConfiguration' fileSpec: | { "files": [ { "pattern": "*.zip" } ] } {code} This resulted in all our .zip files everywhere in artifactory to get this property. Even .zip files that were part of other packages. It is the same functionality if you use the `delete` function instead of `set`. We haven't tested it for obvious reasons, but it appears that using the pattern: {code:java} "pattern": "*.*" {code} Would apply the changes to all artifacts everywhere in artifactory. This means a developer can accidentally, or maliciously, overwrite or delete critical artifact properties, like buildNumber and buildName, for all artifacts that have been uploaded. Even cached artifacts will be changed. For companies who have to work under regulatory conditions this is extremely critical. Naturally it can be reverted from a backup, but that means that you have to be aware that the changes have happened to begin with. I think it is critical that the task is changed so that it should require a repository as a minimum. Ideally it should go even further, so that it is only allowed to add properties and not delete/overwrite existing properties without a user with Admin privileges. Just because a "user" has deploy permissions should not enable them to overwrite/delete properties, only add them.
Comments:
1. [~Dakimaz] Thanks for your feedback. I'll pass it on to our product team. To limit the search results you can set the repository name as part of the pattern. You can also filter, for example, by properties or by build: {code:java} { "files": [ { "pattern": "repoName/*.zip", "props": "keyToFilter=valueToFilter", "build": "buildNameToFilter/buildNumberToFilter" } ] } {code} The File Spec is a powerf

expected.summary: The TFSAP project has a critical bug where artifact property changes can be made without specifying a repository, potentially leading to accidental or malicious overwrites of critical artifact properties.
action_items: Modify the ArtifactoryProperties@1 task to require a repository specification
Restrict the ability to delete or overwrite existing properties to users with Admin privileges
risks: Developers can accidentally or maliciously overwrite or delete critical artifact properties for all artifacts in Artifactory.
Changes made without awareness can lead to compliance issues for companies under regulatory conditions.
